title: Update: Wildfires active in Garden Route area
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS & VIDEOS - In the...
bodyText:
Update
                            Video

GARDEN ROUTE NEWS & VIDEOS - In the latest update from the Garden Route District Municipality, slight rainfall and overcast conditions have been favorable in aiding containment efforts.


> Windmeulnek (Spitskop), Knysna Municipal Area, 27 December - 19:30


The wildfire at Windmeulnek (Spitskop) is currently contained and extinguished on the southern slope. Slight rainfall and overcast conditions have been favorable in aiding containment efforts.

SANParks will continue to monitor the area intermittently, as observations on the northern slopes remain inconclusive.

Should you spot a wildfire, please report it immediately to the GRDM Emergency Call Centre at 044 805 5071 or send a WhatsApp message to 081 709 5193.

VIDEO:

Your browser does not support HTML5 video.


> Kammanassie, George Municipal Area - 27 December @ 20:10


The wildfire in Kammanassie has been contained, with no visible open flames. Weather conditions have been favourable, with light rainfall and overcast clouds assisting efforts.

A night shift crew will remain vigilant and continue monitoring the area throughout the night.

Should you spot a wildfire, please report it immediately to the GRDM Emergency Call Centre at 044 805 5071 or send a WhatsApp message to 081 709 5193.

VIDEO:

Your browser does not support HTML5 video.


> De Kombuys Plaas - Oudtshoorn Municipality


Although the wildfire at Kombuys Plaas is high in the mountains, it is currently under the mist and rain has started in the area. The Greater Oudtshoorn Municipal fire fighters will withdraw and CapeNature will monitor the situation through the night.Oudtshoorn Municipality thanks all the fire fighters and CapeNature for their assistance. Their bravery and dedication is appreciated.

Issued by Oudtshoorn Municipality on 27 December at 20:13


> Jongensfontein, Hessequa Municipal Area, 27 December - 21:00


After the wildfire was contained for several days, a change in the wind to a strong westerly direction caused hot embers to spot from within the burned area, igniting a new wildfire approximately 150 meters away.

This major ignition posed a serious threat to approximately 25 nearby thatch-roof houses. Aerial resources were reactivated, and extensive efforts were required to prevent the wildfire from spreading to structures.

The fire has since been brought under control, and mopping-up operations are currently underway.

Additionally, a counter-burn is being conducted to seal two unburned blocks. Light rain has also begun, and the cooler weather is expected to assist with mopping-up efforts throughout the night.

GRDM Executive Mayor Andrew Stroebel was at the scene for most of the day and would like to extend his thank yous to all the role players for their collective approach to protecting lives and infrastructure. Mayor Stroebel is featured in the video above too, which was taken moments ago.

Should you spot a wildfire, please report it immediately to the GRDM Emergency Call Centre at 044 805 5071 or send a WhatsApp message to 081 709 5193.


> Robinson Pass, Mossel Bay Municipal Area, 27 December - 21:20


This wildfire is declared extinguished. Crews did patrol and mopping up throughout the day, and all lines are cold.

Should you spot a wildfire, please report it immediately to the GRDM Emergency Call Centre at 044 805 5071 or send a WhatsApp message to 081 709 5193.


> Robinson Pass, Mossel Bay Municipal Area, 27 December 2024 - 21:20


This wildfire is declared extinguished. Crews did patrol and mopping up throughout the day, and all lines are cold.

Should you spot a wildfire, please report it immediately to the GRDM Emergency Call Centre at 044 805 5071 or send a WhatsApp message to 081 709 5193.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
